name: cli
context: Terraform CLI commands, global flags, and environment variables for managing infrastructure as code
info: The Terraform CLI is the primary interface for managing infrastructure with HashiCorp Terraform. It provides commands for the full infrastructure lifecycle: initializing working directories (init), previewing changes (plan), applying changes (apply), and tearing down resources (destroy). Beyond the core workflow, the CLI includes commands for state management (state, import, taint), workspace isolation (workspace), output inspection (output, show), formatting (fmt), validation (validate), and module/provider management. Global flags like -chdir, -no-color, and -json control behavior across all commands. Environment variables such as TF_CLI_ARGS, TF_INPUT, TF_IN_AUTOMATION, TF_LOG, and TF_DATA_DIR customize execution without modifying command invocations. Understanding the CLI is essential for both interactive development and CI/CD automation pipelines.
body:
# Terraform CLI

The Terraform CLI is the single binary that drives all infrastructure-as-code operations. It reads `.tf` configuration files written in HCL, communicates with cloud provider APIs through plugins, and manages the state of deployed resources.

## Core Workflow

```
tf init  -->  tf plan  -->  tf apply  -->  (iterate)  -->  tf destroy
   |              |             |                              |
 backend +     compare       create/                      tear down
 providers   state vs config  update/delete               all resources
```

The standard workflow follows four steps:

1. **`terraform init`** — Initialize the working directory, download providers and modules. See [[terraform/cli/init]].
2. **`terraform plan`** — Preview what changes Terraform will make. See [[terraform/cli/plan]].
3. **`terraform apply`** — Execute the changes to reach the desired state. See [[terraform/cli/apply]].
4. **`terraform destroy`** — Tear down all managed infrastructure. See [[terraform/cli/destroy]].

## Installation

```bash
# macOS (Homebrew)
brew install terraform

# Ubuntu/Debian (APT)
wget -O - https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/hashicorp-archive-keyring.gpg] https://apt.releases.hashicorp.com $(lsb_release -cs) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/hashicorp.list
sudo apt update && sudo apt install terraform

# Binary download (any platform)
curl -fsSL https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform/1.9.0/terraform_1.9.0_linux_amd64.zip -o tf.zip
unzip tf.zip && sudo mv terraform /usr/local/bin/

# Verify installation
terraform version
```

## Global Flags

These flags work with every `terraform` subcommand:

| Flag | Purpose | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `-chdir=DIR` | Switch to a different directory before executing | `terraform -chdir=environments/prod plan` |
| `-no-color` | Disable terminal color output (for CI logs) | `terraform plan -no-color` |
| `-json` | Machine-readable JSON output | `terraform plan -json` |
| `-help` | Show usage for any command | `terraform plan -help` |
| `-version` | Print Terraform version | `terraform -version` |

```bash
# Run plan from a different directory
terraform -chdir=modules/vpc plan

# CI-friendly output: no color, JSON format
terraform -no-color plan -json > plan.json
```

## Environment Variables

Environment variables customize CLI behavior without changing command arguments:

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `TF_CLI_ARGS` | Inject extra arguments into every command |
| `TF_CLI_ARGS_plan` | Inject extra arguments into `terraform plan` only |
| `TF_INPUT` | Set to `0` or `false` to disable interactive prompts |
| `TF_IN_AUTOMATION` | Set to any value to adjust output for CI/CD |
| `TF_LOG` | Enable logging: `TRACE`, `DEBUG`, `INFO`, `WARN`, `ERROR` |
| `TF_LOG_PATH` | Write logs to a file instead of stderr |
| `TF_DATA_DIR` | Override default `.terraform` data directory |
| `TF_WORKSPACE` | Select workspace (equivalent to `terraform workspace select`) |
| `TF_VAR_name` | Set variable values: `TF_VAR_region=us-east-1` |
| `TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR` | Shared plugin cache to avoid re-downloading providers |

```bash
# CI/CD environment setup
export TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true
export TF_INPUT=0
export TF_CLI_ARGS_plan="-no-color"
export TF_CLI_ARGS_apply="-no-color -auto-approve"
export TF_LOG=WARN
export TF_LOG_PATH=/var/log/terraform.log

# Shared provider cache across projects
export TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR="$HOME/.terraform.d/plugin-cache"
mkdir -p "$TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR"

# Set variables via environment
export TF_VAR_region=us-east-1
export TF_VAR_instance_type=t3.micro
```

## Additional Commands

Beyond the core workflow, the CLI provides:

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `terraform validate` | Check configuration syntax and internal consistency |
| `terraform fmt` | Reformat `.tf` files to canonical style |
| `terraform show` | Display the current state or a saved plan |
| `terraform output` | Read output values from the state |
| `terraform state list` | List resources tracked in state |
| `terraform state show` | Show attributes of a single resource in state |
| `terraform state mv` | Move a resource in state (rename/refactor) |
| `terraform state rm` | Remove a resource from state without destroying it |
| `terraform import` | Import existing infrastructure into state |
| `terraform workspace` | Create and switch between workspaces |
| `terraform graph` | Generate a DOT dependency graph |
| `terraform taint` | Mark a resource for forced recreation (deprecated; use -replace) |
| `terraform providers` | List providers required by the configuration |
| `terraform console` | Interactive expression evaluation |

```bash
# Validate syntax
terraform validate

# Format all .tf files recursively
terraform fmt -recursive

# Show current state in human-readable form
terraform show

# Read a specific output
terraform output -raw db_endpoint

# Import an existing resource
terraform import aws_s3_bucket.my_bucket my-bucket-name

# Move a resource in state (rename)
terraform state mv aws_instance.old aws_instance.new
```

sections.title: Core workflow
sections.body: The Terraform core workflow is init, plan, apply, destroy. Init downloads providers and modules and configures the backend. Plan compares the current state to the desired configuration and produces an execution plan. Apply executes the plan, creating, updating, or destroying resources. Destroy tears down all managed resources. This workflow applies to both interactive development and CI/CD pipelines.

sections.title: Global flags
sections.body: Global flags modify the behavior of all terraform subcommands. -chdir changes the working directory before execution, letting you run commands against different environments without cd. -no-color strips ANSI color codes from output, which is essential for CI log parsing. -json produces machine-readable JSON output for plan and apply, enabling programmatic consumption of results.

sections.title: Environment variables
sections.body: Terraform reads environment variables to configure behavior without changing commands. TF_CLI_ARGS injects arguments into every invocation. TF_CLI_ARGS_<command> targets a specific subcommand. TF_INPUT=0 disables interactive prompts for CI. TF_IN_AUTOMATION adjusts output formatting. TF_LOG enables debug logging. TF_VAR_<name> sets input variables. TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR shares downloaded providers across projects to save bandwidth and disk.

sections.title: Installation
sections.body: Terraform is distributed as a single static binary. Install via Homebrew on macOS, the HashiCorp APT repository on Debian/Ubuntu, or download the ZIP archive directly from releases.hashicorp.com. Use tfenv or asdf for managing multiple Terraform versions across projects. Verify installation with terraform version.

sections.title: Additional commands
sections.body: Beyond the core workflow, Terraform provides validate for syntax checking, fmt for canonical formatting, show and output for inspecting state, state subcommands for direct state manipulation, import for adopting existing resources, workspace for environment isolation, graph for dependency visualization, console for interactive HCL evaluation, and providers for listing required plugins.

links.context: Initialize working directory, download providers and modules
Apply changes to create, update, or destroy resources

related.note: AWS CloudFormation is an alternative IaC tool specific to AWS
Terraform can manage Docker resources via the Docker provider

scripts.name: Terraform Version Check
scripts.description: Check the installed Terraform version and list available providers in the current directory
scripts.inline: echo '=== Terraform Version ===' && terraform version && echo '' && echo '=== Provider Requirements ===' && if [ -f '*.tf' ] || ls *.tf 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then terraform providers; else echo 'No .tf files in current directory'; fi

scripts.name: CI/CD Environment Setup
scripts.description: Configure environment variables for non-interactive Terraform execution in CI/CD pipelines
scripts.inline:
export TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true
export TF_INPUT=0
export TF_CLI_ARGS_plan="-no-color"
export TF_CLI_ARGS_apply="-no-color -auto-approve"
${TF_LOG:+export TF_LOG=$TF_LOG}
${TF_LOG_PATH:+export TF_LOG_PATH=$TF_LOG_PATH}
echo '=== Terraform CI Environment ===' 
echo "TF_IN_AUTOMATION=$TF_IN_AUTOMATION"
echo "TF_INPUT=$TF_INPUT"
echo "TF_CLI_ARGS_plan=$TF_CLI_ARGS_plan"
echo "TF_CLI_ARGS_apply=$TF_CLI_ARGS_apply"
echo "TF_LOG=${TF_LOG:-<unset>}"
terraform version

TF_LOG.description: Log level: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR

scripts.name: Full Terraform Workflow
scripts.description: Run the complete init-validate-plan-apply workflow in sequence
scripts.inline:
set -euo pipefail
DIR="${TF_WORKING_DIR:-.}"
echo '=== Step 1: Init ===' && terraform -chdir="$DIR" init -input=false
echo '' && echo '=== Step 2: Validate ===' && terraform -chdir="$DIR" validate
echo '' && echo '=== Step 3: Plan ===' && terraform -chdir="$DIR" plan -input=false -out=tfplan
if [ "${AUTO_APPLY:-false}" = 'true' ]; then
  echo '' && echo '=== Step 4: Apply ===' && terraform -chdir="$DIR" apply -input=false tfplan
else
  echo '' && echo 'Plan saved to tfplan. Set AUTO_APPLY=true to apply automatically.'
fi

TF_WORKING_DIR.description: Working directory containing .tf files (default: current directory)

AUTO_APPLY.description: Set to 'true' to auto-apply after plan (default: false)

chdir.description: Change to this directory before executing

depends.reason: Terraform must be installed to use the CLI

frontmatter.author: dojo-community
frontmatter.audience: developers, AI agents, DevOps engineers, SREs
